ogTitle: Sunnyside vs Sunset Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunnyside and Sunset Beach.
heading: Sunnyside vs Sunset Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunnyside, Western Australia and Sunset Beach, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunset Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Sunnyside (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunnyside or Sunset Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunnyside has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Sunset Beach scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunnyside scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Sunset Beach scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunnyside or Sunset Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunset Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
